task_updates.executor_notes: Removed `target_timeline_frame` from the duplicate hook input and RPC payload, updated the duplicate-frame comment, confirmed the existing duplicate hook test remains valid, and verified `rg -n "target_timeline_frame" src` returns no results.
Removed the three TimelineEditor debug console.log calls and verified `rg -n "\[ShotCreate" .` returns no results anywhere in the repo.

Extracted `usePortalMousedownGuard` with JSDoc documenting the `createPortal`/`Node.contains()` root cause and replaced the three duplicated portal-menu mousedown guards. Also updated the TimelineCanvas test mock so the full TimelineEditor suite still passes.

Simplified `useAddImageToShot` to an invalidation-only flow: removed `onMutate`, temp-ID replacement, and rollback logic; kept the DB mutation path; added `enqueueGenerationsInvalidation` plus explicit `segments.liveTimeline`, `segments.parents`, and `generations.meta` invalidations; removed `skipOptimistic` from the shot-add variables and related call sites; and added a verification test for the new invalidation contract.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Verified the new migration drops the 6-arg overload, creates the 5-arg function, and reapplies GRANT EXECUTE plus COMMENT.
Verified `rg -n "target_timeline_frame" src` returns no results after the TypeScript hook/comment update.
Verified `rg -n "\[ShotCreate" .` returns no results after removing the debug logs.
Verified the new hook JSDoc explains the `createPortal`/`Node.contains()` root cause and all three portal menus now call `usePortalMousedownGuard`.
Verified the simplified `addImageToShot` success path includes `enqueueGenerationsInvalidation` plus explicit invalidations for `segments.liveTimeline`, `segments.parents`, and `generations.meta`.
